content: I'm thinking about starting a small side business selling my hand-knit scarves. I've been knitting for about 5 years now, ever since my daughter gave me a beginner's kit for my birthday.
That sounds like a wonderful idea! What made you decide to turn your knitting hobby into a business now?
Well, I'm finishing my MBA part-time at Arizona State and one of my classes is about entrepreneurship. The professor keeps saying we should test our business ideas, so I figured why not?
That's a great practical application of your coursework! What kind of scarves do you make?
Mostly wool and alpaca blends. I source the yarn from a local shop in Phoenix called 'The Twisted Yarn.' They have these amazing merino wool skeins that are so soft.
Sounds luxurious! How do you price your work?
I've been tracking my time and materials. A basic scarf takes me about 8 hours and uses 2 skeins of yarn. I was thinking of charging around $80-90, which seems fair for handmade items.
That pricing makes sense for handmade goods. Have you thought about where you'd sell them?
I was considering Etsy to start, but I also go to the farmer's market in Scottsdale every Saturday. The organizer said I could set up a booth for $30 per week.
The farmer's market sounds like a great way to get local exposure! What's your MBA focus?
Healthcare administration. I work as a physical therapist specializing in sports injuries at a clinic in Tempe. The MBA is helping me understand the business side of healthcare better.
That's a fascinating combination! Do you find time for your other hobbies?

Honestly, it's a juggling act. I try to practice archery twice a week at the range near Papago Park. There's something meditative about it. And I've been getting into chess more seriously - joined an online club that meets Thursday evenings.
You're quite the Renaissance person! How do you balance everything?
Coffee helps. I've become a bit of a coffee snob actually - I roast my own beans on the weekends. There's a small shop in downtown Phoenix that sells green beans from all over the world.
That's impressive! Coffee roasting is quite an art. Do you have a favorite origin?
I'm partial to Ethiopian Yirgacheffe. The floral notes are amazing. Speaking of hobbies, I've been thinking about taking up yoga too, but I'm not sure if I can fit it in.
Yoga could be a nice complement to your other activities. Maybe you could try some online classes during lunch breaks?
That's not a bad idea. I usually work through lunch at the clinic, but maybe I should start taking that break. My daughter keeps telling me I need to slow down.
She sounds wise! How old is your daughter?
She just turned 25. She's a graphic designer in San Francisco. We talk almost every weekend - she's been giving me branding advice for the scarf business.
That's wonderful that you're so close! It sounds like you have a lot of exciting projects going on. How's the scarf business coming along?

I've made about 15 scarves so far and have three pre-orders from friends at work. I'm planning to launch my Etsy shop next month once I finish my current MBA project. Just need to take photos and write descriptions.
That's fantastic progress! You should be proud of how much you're accomplishing. Keep me posted on how the launch goes!
